summary:
The patient meets criteria for prior rabies vaccination due to receiving complete series previously; therefore, post-exposure prophylaxis requires just one dose administered at day 0 if not given before, per current guidelines—no need for rabies immune globulin since there’s no sign of immunodeficiency or severe risk factors present.

Final Summary:  
The child qualifies as having completed preexposure rabies vaccinations, so post-exposure management involves administering a single dose of rabies vaccine at Day 0 without needing rabies immunoglobulin unless indicated by specific risks like immunosuppression—which are absent here.
